task objective: Validate and address the maintainability findings in docs/review/2026-06-07_thermo_nuclear_review.md, then publish a replacement development release.
tests added: SyncthingWatch._tick accepts only self and now because runtime polling state is instance-owned.
The activity RPC sample exposes only the seven fields consumed by the frontend.
SyncthingMonitor.start returns an opaque watch session with cancel and post-game handoff methods and no generation identifier.
design decisions: Finding 1 was valid: mutable watcher state now belongs to SyncthingWatch and the tuple-return plumbing was deleted.
Finding 2 was valid: lifecycle consumers now receive a SyncthingWatchSession instead of the monitor's internal generation key.
Finding 3 was valid with a lifecycle caveat: finally blocks cancel sessions by default, while successful pre-game retention and post-game handoff explicitly transfer ownership to the monitor.
Finding 4 was valid: one createEpochGuardedSurface factory replaces duplicate start and exit closures.

Finding 5 was directionally valid although the payload had 16 rather than over 20 fields: nine unused diagnostic fields were removed from the RPC contract while internal diagnostics remain available for activity computation.
results: watcher.py decreased from 436 lines to 323 lines.
gameLifecycleController.tsx decreased from 730 lines to 704 lines and now has three cohesive session cancellation points.
488 Python tests and 52 frontend tests pass.
Ruff, formatting, ty, TypeScript type checking, frontend verification, Rollup build, and package validation pass.
